relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Stumbling-blocks come, but causing them carries woe.
The little ones must be protected from being made to stumble.
Watchfulness includes rebuke, turning, and release.
Repeated return calls for repeated release.
The apostles ask for added trust.
Mustard-seed trust can uproot what seems fixed.
Service continues after the field.
The servant prepares and serves before resting.
Commanded service is not performed for applause.
Humility says: we have done what was ours to do.
The Jerusalem road passes through Samaria and Galilee.
Ten afflicted men stand at a distance.
Their cry asks mercy.
On the way to the priests, cleansing happens.
One sees the healing and returns in glory.
The grateful one falls at Jesus’ feet, and he is Samaritan.
Jesus asks where the nine are.
The outsider becomes the returning worshiper.
Trust saves/heals and sends him onward.
The reign does not arrive as spectacle to be tracked.
The reign is within and among, already present in their midst.
The disciples will long for days of the Son of Humanity.
Do not chase claims of here or there.
The Son of Humanity’s day will be as unmistakable as lightning.
But first comes suffering and rejection.
The days of Noah become a pattern.
Ordinary life continued until sudden flood.
The days of Lot also show ordinary commerce and building.
Then sudden judgment fell after Lot left.
So too the Son of Humanity is revealed.
Urgency leaves no time to retrieve possessions.
Remember the cost of turning back.
Life grasped is lost; life surrendered is kept alive.
Nearness does not guarantee the same outcome.
Shared work still meets separation.
A later manuscript repeats the taken-left image in a field.
The gathered birds mark where the body is.
